prompt: The DatagramSocket send throws IllegalArgumentException. What is the bug?
choices: DatagramSocket.send requires the socket to be connected first
DatagramPacket must be created with a destination InetAddress and port; without them the packet has no target and send throws IllegalArgumentException
"hello".getBytes() must specify a charset to avoid encoding issues
DatagramSocket cannot send packets unless it is bound to a specific port
explanation: DatagramPacket(byte[], int) creates a packet for receiving. To send, you must use DatagramPacket(byte[], int, InetAddress, int) which includes the destination address and port. Calling send() on a packet without an address throws IllegalArgumentException. Fix: DatagramPacket packet = new DatagramPacket(data, data.length, InetAddress.getByName("host"), port);
